channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Man arrested in plot to firebomb Palestinian activist's home after undercover op
item.description: An official who was briefed on the investigation said Alexander Heifler, 26, identified as a member of the JDL 613 Brotherhood, which describes its membership as "Jewish warriors" fighting back against rising antisemitism.
item.content:encoded:
An official who was briefed on the investigation said Alexander Heifler, 26, identified as a member of the JDL 613 Brotherhood, which describes its membership as "Jewish warriors" fighting back against rising antisemitism.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tiger Woods arrested on suspicion of DUI, authorities say
item.description: Woods was arrested on suspicion of DUI after he struck another vehicle and rolled his Land Rover, not far from where he lives on Jupiter Island, Florida. The sheriff's office said he was not injured.
item.content:encoded:
Woods was arrested on suspicion of DUI after he struck another vehicle and rolled his Land Rover, not far from where he lives on Jupiter Island, Florida. The sheriff's office said he was not injured.

(Image credit: Reinhold Matay)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hegseth blocked 2 Black and 2 female soldiers from promotions
item.description: Pete Hegseth has reportedly blocked the military promotion of six officers: all women or Black men, to become one-star generals.
item.content:encoded: Pete Hegseth has reportedly blocked the military promotion of six officers: all women or Black men, to become one-star generals.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Leqaa Kordia is free now, after a year in ICE detention
item.description: In her own words, we hear from a young Palestinian woman from Paterson, N.J., who was released from ICE detention in Texas. She had been held for over a year.
item.content:encoded: In her own words, we hear from a young Palestinian woman from Paterson, N.J., who was released from ICE detention in Texas. She had been held for over a year.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: Atlanta airport GM says passengers have been cooperative and understanding
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Ricky Smith, general manager of Atlanta's Hartsfield-Jackson airport, about how TSA agents and passengers are faring amid the partial government shutdown.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Ricky Smith, general manager of Atlanta's Hartsfield-Jackson airport, about how TSA agents and passengers are faring amid the partial government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Acovino

item.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth intervened to stop promotions of Black and female officers
item.description: Four Army officers were on track to become one-star generals, NPR confirms. Defense secretary Pete Hegseth's involvement in the promotion process is highly unusual.
item.content:encoded:
Four Army officers were on track to become one-star generals, NPR confirms. Defense secretary Pete Hegseth's involvement in the promotion process is highly unusual.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: Americans seek redemption at figure skating worlds, just weeks after the Olympics
item.description: Figure Skating World Championships in Prague end on Saturday. Americans Amber Glenn and Ilia Malinin are within medals' reach after disappointing finishes at last month's Olympics.
item.content:encoded:
Figure Skating World Championships in Prague end on Saturday. Americans Amber Glenn and Ilia Malinin are within medals' reach after disappointing finishes at last month's Olympics.

(Image credit: Michal Cizek)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Iranian missile hits an airbase in Saudi Arabia, injuring U.S. troops
item.description: A U.S. official said several U.S. service members were wounded in the strike and some aircraft were apparently damaged as well. Iran has targeted U.S. bases throughout the region since the war began.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. official said several U.S. service members were wounded in the strike and some aircraft were apparently damaged as well. Iran has targeted U.S. bases throughout the region since the war began.

(Image credit: Fatemeh Bahrami)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Our quiz writer made yet another Tom Bombadil reference this week. Can you spot it?
item.description: How well do you know your "Lord of the Rings"? What about AI, Washington landmarks and TSA wait times? Find out!
item.content:encoded: How well do you know your "Lord of the Rings"? What about AI, Washington landmarks and TSA wait times? Find out!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump delays deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Senate votes overnight to fund much of DHS but not ICE, Iran war tests loyalty of Trump's base at this year's CPAC.
item.content:encoded: President Trump delays deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Senate votes overnight to fund much of DHS but not ICE, Iran war tests loyalty of Trump's base at this year's CPAC.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
